definition: A meeting to decide if someone gets a job
example: I have a job interview tomorrow.

definition: Money you earn from your job each month
example: The salary for this position is competitive.

definition: A person you work with
example: My colleagues are very supportive.

definition: The latest time something must be finished
example: The deadline for the report is Friday.

definition: A planned event where people discuss things
example: We have a meeting at 10 a.m.

definition: Knowledge gained from doing a job over time
example: This job requires three years of experience.

definition: An ability to do something well
example: Communication skills are important.

definition: To formally ask for a job
example: I want to apply for this position.

definition: To give someone a job
example: They want to hire a new designer.

definition: To officially leave your job
example: She resigned from her position last month.

definition: To give someone a better job with more responsibility
example: He was promoted to manager.

definition: To travel to and from work
example: I commute for an hour each day.

definition: Working the whole working week
example: She has a full-time job.

definition: Working only part of the week
example: He has a part-time job at the cafe.

definition: Related to a job that needs special training
example: She is very professional in her work.

definition: Working hours beyond your normal schedule
example: I had to work overtime last week.

definition: Something extra you get from your job (health insurance, etc.)
example: This job has great benefits.

definition: A group of people working together
example: Our team finished the project early.

definition: A piece of work that needs to be done
example: I have several tasks to complete today.

definition: A document listing your education and work experience
example: Please send your resume by email.
